newsSince: No new confirmed fundamentals since June 9: Micron reports Q3 FY2026 on June 24 (after this eval), so the +15.3% move is pre-earnings run-up. Pre-print commentary reaffirms HBM sold out through 2026 with pricing locked, HBM4 36GB 12-Hi in volume production for Nvidia Vera Rubin, ~$500/stack (+~30% vs HBM3E), and tightness expected beyond CY2026; sell-side expects record ~81% gross margin. Thesis intact/strengthening but the print itself is an unrealized catalyst and risk.

verdicts.rankRationale: On the passives/power-bottleneck lens MU stays peripheral: HBM/memory is direct-AI, not passive-component scarcity. Pre-earnings hype and reaffirmed sold-out pricing don't move this page's lens; a $1.2T mega-cap remains far from hidden. Hold.
verdicts.bull: Sold-out HBM through 2026, locked pricing, HBM4 ramp; durable memory cash flows.
verdicts.neutral: Real AI exposure but tangential to passive/power scarcity basket.
verdicts.bear: Mega-cap, fully discovered; June 24 print could disappoint vs ~81% GM bar.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: June 24 Q3 print / HBM4 revenue mix

verdicts.rankRationale: Node-centrality reaffirmed: HBM sold out 2026, pricing locked, HBM4 in volume for Vera Rubin, tightness beyond CY2026. Reinforces but doesn't extend the Jun 5/9 case; the June 24 print is an unrealized catalyst, so +1 not a move at rank 18.
verdicts.bull: HBM4 volume ramp + locked pricing make MU a structural AI-node bottleneck winner.
verdicts.neutral: Most positives already captured at rank 18; print not yet realized.
verdicts.bear: Rubin memory-config-cut volume risk; cyclical pricing peak.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: HBM4 revenue contribution at Q3 print

verdicts.rankRationale: On the co-packaged-optics lens MU is only indirect via larger AI clusters; no CPO-specific catalyst since June 9. HBM strength helps cluster scale but not optical content. Hold near bottom.
verdicts.bull: Bigger AI clusters need more memory, indirectly lifting optical interconnect.
verdicts.neutral: No direct CPO content; relevance second-order.
verdicts.bear: Zero co-packaged-optics exposure; ranked appropriately low.

verdicts.rankRationale: Master under-owned-bottleneck list: HBM & AI-memory bucket thesis intact, with reaffirmed sold-out/locked pricing and HBM4 ramp. Supplier-execution watch-item now de-risking ahead of June 24, warranting a small +1; not enough for a rank move on a $1.2T discovered name.
verdicts.bull: Scarce HBM bottleneck with sold-out capacity and locked pricing into 2026.
verdicts.neutral: Fundamentally strong but heavily owned and re-rated.
verdicts.bear: Rubin memory-config overhang; pricing-cycle risk at print.
